deviations.description: The new requirement in Version 2 obligates assessment only if the donor is still bleeding after 20 minutes, whereas the process model (see diagram on page 1) triggers assessment and recording after 15 minutes if the donor is still bleeding or experiencing pain. The process model does not enforce the new 20-minute threshold and does not restrict the trigger to bleeding only, resulting in a violation of the stricter timing and condition in Version 2.
process_model_reference: Patient Monitoring, Event based gateway (15 Minutes Timeout), Assess donor

deviations.description: The process model continues to require assessment and recording for both bleeding and pain after 15 minutes, and also obligates recording the reason in the system, which is no longer required in Version 2. This results in unnecessary actions beyond the new requirement.

The process model enforces assessment and recording after 15 minutes, while Version 2 only requires assessment after 20 minutes if the donor is still bleeding. The process model is stricter than necessary regarding both timing and conditions.
process_model_reference: Event based gateway (15 Minutes Timeout), Record observations
